modules.title: JS Core Concepts

sections.heading: var vs let vs const
sections.content: var is function-scoped and hoisted. let and const are block-scoped. const cannot be reassigned but objects/arrays it holds CAN be mutated.
sections.tip: Always use const by default. Use let only when reassigning. Never use var.

sections.heading: Hoisting
sections.content: JavaScript moves declarations to the top before execution. var is hoisted as undefined. let/const are hoisted but not initialized (Temporal Dead Zone).
sections.tip: Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ) is a favorite interview question.

sections.heading: Scope & Closures
sections.content: A closure is when a function remembers variables from its outer scope even after the outer function has returned. Most important JS concept.
sections.tip: setTimeout loop is asked in 90% of JS interviews.

sections.heading: this Keyword
sections.content: this depends on HOW a function is called, not where defined. Arrow functions inherit this from surrounding scope.
sections.tip: Arrow functions dont have own this. Perfect for callbacks, wrong for object methods.

sections.heading: Type Coercion
sections.content: == does type coercion. === does not. Always use ===.
sections.tip: ALWAYS use ===. If interviewer sees ==, instant red flag.

key_points: const by default, let for reassign, never var
this depends on HOW called, not WHERE defined

module_quiz.question: var x=1; if(true){var x=2;} console.log(x)?
module_quiz.explanation: var is function-scoped. Inner var overwrites outer.

module_quiz.question: What is Temporal Dead Zone?
options: Zone where let/const hoisted but cant access
module_quiz.explanation: let/const are hoisted but uninitialized before declaration line.

module_quiz.question: for(var i=0;i<3;i++){setTimeout(()=>console.log(i),100)}?
module_quiz.explanation: var is function-scoped. Loop finishes, all see i=3.

module_quiz.question: Arrow functions have own this?
module_quiz.explanation: Arrow functions capture this from surrounding lexical scope.
=== strict — different types = false.

modules.title: Arrays & Objects Deep

sections.heading: map, filter, reduce
sections.content: The three most important array methods. map transforms, filter selects, reduce combines.
sections.code:
const nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
nums.map(n => n * 2);       // [2,4,6,8,10]
nums.filter(n => n % 2===0); // [2,4]
nums.reduce((a,b) => a+b, 0); // 15

// Chain
const result = nums
    .filter(n => n > 2)
    .map(n => n * 10)
    .reduce((a,b) => a+b);  // 120
sections.tip: Chaining map/filter/reduce = mark of senior developer.

sections.heading: Destructuring & Spread
sections.content: Destructuring extracts values. Spread copies or merges.
sections.tip: Destructuring in function params: function({name, age}) {}

sections.heading: More Array Methods
sections.content: find, some, every, includes, flat, sort, slice vs splice.
sections.code:
arr.find(n => n > 4);     // First match
arr.some(n => n > 8);     // true if any pass
arr.every(n => n > 0);    // true if all pass
arr.flat(Infinity);       // Flatten nested

// CAUTION: sort mutates!
arr.sort((a,b) => a-b);   // Ascending
// slice returns copy, splice mutates
sections.tip: sort() without comparator converts to strings! Always pass (a,b)=>a-b.

sections.heading: Object Methods
sections.content: Keys, values, entries, optional chaining, nullish coalescing.
sections.code:
Object.keys(obj);   // ['name','age']
Object.values(obj);  // ['Alice', 25]
Object.entries(obj); // [['name','Alice'],['age',25]]

data?.user?.name;   // Safe deep access
value ?? 'default';  // Only null/undefined trigger
0 ?? 'default';      // 0 (not null)
0 || 'default';      // 'default' (0 is falsy)
sections.tip: ?? checks null/undefined only. || checks all falsy.

key_points: map transforms, filter selects, reduce combines
sort() without comparator converts to strings

module_quiz.question: [1,2,3].map(n=>n*2)?
module_quiz.explanation: map transforms each element.

module_quiz.question: const {name} = {name:'Alice'}; name?
module_quiz.explanation: Destructuring extracts name property.

module_quiz.question: [10,9,8].sort()?
module_quiz.explanation: Converts to strings. '10'<'8' alphabetically.

module_quiz.question: 0 ?? 'default'?
module_quiz.explanation: ?? only triggers on null/undefined. 0 is valid.

module_quiz.question: data?.user?.name if user undefined?
module_quiz.explanation: Optional chaining returns undefined, not error.

sections.heading: Callbacks to Promises
sections.content: Callbacks create pyramid code. Promises flatten it into chains.
sections.tip: Callback hell solved by Promises and async/await.

sections.heading: Async/Await
sections.content: Syntactic sugar over Promises. Makes async look synchronous.
sections.tip: await in loop is sequential. Use Promise.all for parallel.

sections.heading: Event Loop
sections.content: How JS handles async despite being single-threaded.
sections.code:
console.log('1');           // Sync
setTimeout(() => console.log('2'), 0);  // Macro
Promise.resolve().then(() => console.log('3'));  // Micro
console.log('4');           // Sync
// Output: 1, 4, 3, 2
sections.tip: Sync first, then microtasks (Promises), then macrotasks (setTimeout).

module_quiz.question: log(1); setTimeout(()=>log(2),0); Promise.then(()=>log(3)); log(4)?
module_quiz.explanation: Sync(1,4), micro/Promise(3), macro/setTimeout(2).

module_quiz.question: Promise.all fails if?
module_quiz.explanation: Rejects if any single promise rejects.

module_quiz.question: async function returns?
module_quiz.explanation: Always returns Promise.

module_quiz.question: await in for loop?
module_quiz.explanation: Each await pauses loop. Use Promise.all for parallel.

module_quiz.question: Microtask example?
module_quiz.explanation: Promise callbacks are microtasks.

modules.title: DOM & Events

sections.heading: DOM Manipulation
sections.content: Select, modify, create, remove elements.
sections.tip: textContent is safe. innerHTML can execute malicious scripts.

sections.heading: Events
sections.content: Bubbling, delegation, preventDefault.
sections.tip: Event delegation = senior pattern. Works for dynamic elements.

key_points: textContent safe, innerHTML has XSS risk

module_quiz.question: textContent vs innerHTML?
module_quiz.explanation: innerHTML parses HTML — XSS risk. textContent is plain text.

module_quiz.question: Event bubbling?
module_quiz.explanation: Events bubble UP from child to parent elements.

module_quiz.question: preventDefault()?
module_quiz.explanation: Prevents default like form submit page reload.

module_quiz.question: Event delegation advantage?
options: One listener for all children + dynamic
module_quiz.explanation: Parent catches all child events via bubbling.

module_quiz.question: querySelector('.card') returns?
module_quiz.explanation: Returns FIRST match. querySelectorAll for all.

sections.heading: Template Literals & Modules
sections.content: Backtick strings with expressions. ES modules for code organization.
sections.tip: Default can be imported with any name. Named must use exact names.

sections.heading: Classes
sections.content: Sugar over prototypes. Used in React and Node.
sections.tip: Private fields use # prefix: #name.

sections.heading: Generators & Symbols
sections.content: Generators yield values lazily. Symbols are unique identifiers.
sections.tip: Generators compute only when asked — perfect for pagination.

module_quiz.question: `Hello ${2+3}`?
module_quiz.explanation: Template literals evaluate expressions.

module_quiz.question: Default exports per file?
module_quiz.explanation: One default, unlimited named.

module_quiz.question: super() in child constructor?
module_quiz.explanation: super() required before accessing this.

module_quiz.question: function* is?
module_quiz.explanation: Generator that yields values lazily.

module_quiz.question: Symbol()===Symbol()?
module_quiz.explanation: Every Symbol is unique.

modules.title: Interview Questions & Answers

sections.heading: Event Delegation
sections.content: One listener on parent instead of many on children.
sections.tip: Works for dynamically added elements too.

sections.heading: Debounce vs Throttle
sections.content: Debounce waits until stop. Throttle limits rate.
sections.tip: Debounce = search input. Throttle = scroll/resize.

sections.heading: null vs undefined
sections.content: undefined = not assigned. null = intentionally empty.
sections.code:
let x;          // undefined
let y = null;   // null
typeof null     // 'object' (historic bug!)
null == undefined  // true
null === undefined // false
sections.tip: typeof null === 'object' is a famous 1995 JS bug.

sections.heading: Promise Methods
sections.content: all, allSettled, race — different behaviors.
sections.code:
Promise.all([p1,p2])       // Fails if ANY rejects
Promise.allSettled([p1,p2]) // Returns all results
Promise.race([p1,p2])       // First to settle wins
sections.tip: all for must-succeed. allSettled for all results. race for timeouts.

key_points: Debounce for input, throttle for scroll
Promise.all fails fast, allSettled completes all

module_quiz.question: typeof null?
module_quiz.explanation: Famous bug — returns 'object'.

module_quiz.question: Debounce best for?
module_quiz.explanation: Waits until user stops typing.

module_quiz.question: allSettled vs all?
module_quiz.explanation: all fails on first reject. allSettled always completes.

module_quiz.question: Prototype chain ends at?
module_quiz.explanation: Chain: obj → proto → Object.prototype → null.

module_quiz.question: let x; console.log(x)?
module_quiz.explanation: Declared but not assigned = undefined.

modules.title: Error Handling & Debugging

sections.heading: Custom Errors
sections.content: Production code needs typed errors for proper handling.
sections.tip: instanceof lets you handle different error types differently.

sections.heading: Pro Debugging
sections.content: Beyond console.log — use table, time, group, debugger.
sections.tip: console.table for objects, console.time for performance.

key_points: Custom Error classes for different types
console.table, time, group for pro debugging
Never show raw errors to users

module_quiz.question: Custom error extends?
module_quiz.explanation: Extend built-in Error for stack traces.

module_quiz.question: instanceof checks?
module_quiz.explanation: Checks if object is instance of a class.

module_quiz.question: console.table best for?
module_quiz.explanation: Displays array of objects as formatted table.

module_quiz.question: debugger does?
module_quiz.explanation: Pauses at that line when DevTools is open.

module_quiz.question: Why custom error classes?
module_quiz.explanation: instanceof allows type-specific error handling.

modules.title: Functional Programming Patterns

sections.heading: Pure Functions & Immutability
sections.content: Pure = same input same output, no side effects. Essential for React.
sections.tip: React state REQUIRES immutability. Never mutate directly.

sections.heading: Currying & Memoization
sections.content: Currying chains single-arg functions. Memoization caches results.
sections.tip: Memoization is the concept behind React.memo and useMemo.

key_points: Immutability: spread to create new copies

module_quiz.question: Pure function modifies globals?
module_quiz.explanation: Pure functions have zero side effects.

module_quiz.question: multiply(2) returns function — this is?
module_quiz.explanation: Currying: multi-arg becomes chain of single-arg.

module_quiz.question: Memoization does?
module_quiz.explanation: Stores results. Same input = cached result.

module_quiz.question: Immutable array add?
module_quiz.explanation: Spread creates new array. push mutates original.

module_quiz.question: Higher-order function?
module_quiz.explanation: map, filter, reduce are higher-order functions.

modules.title: Performance & Memory

sections.heading: Memory Leaks
sections.content: Forgotten listeners, closures holding data, uncleaned timers.
sections.tip: In React, cleanup in useEffect return function.

sections.heading: Optimization
sections.content: Batch DOM ops, use DocumentFragment, debounce events.
sections.tip: DOM manipulation is slowest. Minimize reads/writes.

key_points: Closures can keep large data alive

module_quiz.question: Common JS memory leak?
module_quiz.explanation: Listeners keep references alive.

module_quiz.question: DocumentFragment advantage?
module_quiz.explanation: Build off-screen, append once.

module_quiz.question: Web Workers for?
module_quiz.explanation: Background thread keeps UI responsive.

module_quiz.question: clearInterval prevents?
module_quiz.explanation: Uncleaned timers keep running and leak.

module_quiz.question: Layout thrashing?
module_quiz.explanation: Alternating reads/writes forces recalculation.

sections.heading: Module & Observer
sections.content: Module = private state + public API. Observer = subscribe to events.
sections.tip: Observer powers React state, Redux, Node EventEmitter.

sections.heading: Singleton, Factory, Proxy
sections.content: Singleton = one instance. Factory = create by type. Proxy = intercept operations.
sections.tip: Proxy powers Vue.js reactivity system.

module_quiz.question: Module pattern achieves?
module_quiz.explanation: Closures create private variables.

module_quiz.question: Observer used in?
module_quiz.explanation: Foundation of event-driven architecture.

module_quiz.question: Singleton ensures?
module_quiz.explanation: One instance for shared resources.

module_quiz.question: Proxy can?
module_quiz.explanation: Intercepts get, set, delete on objects.

module_quiz.question: Factory advantage?
options: Creates objects by type without specifying class
module_quiz.explanation: Centralizes creation logic.

quiz.question: const arr=[1,2]; arr.push(3); Error?
options: No — push mutates, const blocks reassign

quiz.question: setTimeout loop with var?
quiz.explanation: var function-scoped, all see final i

quiz.question: Debounce vs throttle?
options: Debounce waits stop, throttle limits rate

quiz.question: Event delegation?
options: One parent listener for all children

quiz.question: Pure function side effects?
quiz.explanation: No side effects, same in same out

quiz.question: typeof NaN?
quiz.explanation: NaN is type number. Use Number.isNaN()
